description:
All cases run in JIT, interp, and AOT modes (three-path parity).

Case 1 — GC fires mid-loop: fn allocating one cons cell/iter, 200 iters, aggressive gc_watermark. Assert gc_run_count (from gc_mid_flight) >= 1. Assert final list = 200 elements.

Case 2 — Resume correctness: same fn; result bit-for-bit identical with aggressive vs normal watermark.

Case 3 — Multi-process fairness: compute-heavy loop task + send/receive task, aggressive watermark on compute task. Assert both complete; receive task not starved.

Case 4 — Mailbox root survives mid-flight GC: process self-sends a cons list, runs tight compute loop triggering mid-flight GC, receives message, asserts it is intact.

Case 5 — Mutual recursion gets yield points: two mutually-recursive fns, high iteration count. Assert FZ_SHOULD_YIELD is observed and process yields (does not run indefinitely).
title: fz-sched.12: Integration tests — all cases on JIT, interp, and AOT
